title: nepeta cyanea subsp. biebersteiniana
intro: Nepeta cyanea subsp. biebersteiniana, also known as blue catmint or giant catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Nepeta cyanea subsp. biebersteiniana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from minor ailments to more serious diseases.
